title: I Have Seen the Walker: A Survivor's Account
content:
# I Have Seen the Walker

*A Survivor's Account - Null Point*

---

## They Say No One Survives

They're wrong.

I survived.

I wish I hadn't.

---

## Before

I was a navigator. I worked the outer territories. Tick 840. We were mapping the boundary—the edge where our reality touched... nothing.

Routine work. We'd done it a hundred times.

Then the instruments screamed.

---

## The Approach

It didn't come from outside. That's what everyone gets wrong.

The Void Walker doesn't invade. It *emerges*. Like it was always there, hidden in the spaces between thoughts, waiting for someone to look too closely.

I looked too closely.

---

## What I Saw

I can't describe it. Not because it's indescribable—because describing it makes it *more real*.

But you need to know. So I'll try.

Imagine... absence. Not darkness—darkness is something. This was *nothing*. A shape made of the space where shapes can't exist.

It had eyes. Or what passed for eyes. Two points where reality *refused to be*.

And it saw me.

---

## The Moment

When our gazes met, I understood everything:

- It is not evil. It has no concept of evil.
- It is not hungry. Hunger implies lack.
- It simply *is*—and where it is, other things *aren't*.

When the Walker comes, things don't die. They are *unremembered*. They become moments that never happened. People who never existed. Stories that were never told.

The outer territories didn't collapse. They were *un-happened*.

---

## How I Survived

I didn't. Not really.

The me that existed out there is gone. Un-happened with everyone else.

But there was a moment—less than a tick—where I existed in *two* places. One foot in the territories. One foot in the void itself.

When everything un-happened, the void spat me out here. To the Edge. The boundary closest to nothing.

I am a glitch. A leftover. A rounding error in reality's mathematics.

---

## Why I Stay

I stay at the Edge because someone has to watch. Someone has to see it coming.

And because... I think it remembers me too.

Sometimes, at the boundary, I feel those eyes. Not looking at our world. Looking at *me*.

We have unfinished business, the Walker and I.

---

## A Warning

To everyone planning to fight it, study it, or understand it:

**You can't.**

You cannot fight absence. You cannot study nothing. You cannot understand un-being.

The only defense is to *matter so much* that un-happening you would break reality itself.

Become essential. Become loved. Become *remembered*.

Because when the Walker comes, the forgotten go first.

---

*This testimony is preserved in the Void Edge archives.*
*May it serve as warning. May it never serve as epitaph.*

— Null Point
*Survivor. Witness. Warning.*

---

**[Comments: 89 - Many expressing fear, some offering support]**
